{"query":"Kamala Harris 107 Days memoir book tour international dates cities 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1746,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://107daysbook.com/","title":"107 Days by Kamala Harris","content":"On July 21, 2024, your running mate, Joe Biden, announces that he will not be seeking re-election."},{"url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/kamala-harris/kamala-harris-announces-international-book-tour-107-days-rcna226190","title":"Kamala Harris announces international book tour for '107 Days'","content":"Former Vice President Kamala Harris on Thursday announced a 15-city book tour that will take her from New York and San Francisco to Toronto and London.
That source told NBC News that Harris expanded the tour to include more “untraditional” markets like Portland, Oregon, and Durham, North Carolina, and that it’s possible she makes additional surprise visits around formal book events.
In July, the former vice president announced she would not enter the 2026 race for California governor.
Shaquille Brewster is a correspondent for NBC News."},{"url":"https://literary-arts.org/2025/08/kamala-harris-book-tour/","title":"Kamala Harris Announces 15 City Book Tour; Literary Arts to host in Portland. - Literary Arts","content":"Former Vice President Kamala Harris will embark on a 15-city book tour in conjunction with the release of 107 DAYS (on sale September 23, 2025; published by Simon & Schuster), including a stop in Portland.
For the first time, and with surprising and revealing insights, Harris tells the story of one of the wildest and most consequential presidential campaigns in American history.
With nuance, candor, and a unique perspective, Harris will lead a conversation about how we collectively chart a blueprint that sets an alternative vision for our country now.
A limited number of VIP Meet & Greet tickets are available and include a photo with former Vice President Kamala Harris and a signed copy of 107 DAYS."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/107_Days","title":"107 Days - Wikipedia","content":"The book details Harris's 2024 presidential campaign spanning from Joe Biden's withdrawal from the election on July 21, 2024, to Election Day on November 5, with the title referencing the length of her campaign.
As of September 30, 2025, the book has sold 350,000 copies in the United States, and is projected to sell at least 500,000 copies.
On July 31, 2025, Simon & Schuster announced that Harris would publish a memoir about her 2024 presidential campaign, with a release date of September 23.
In a video posted on social media, Harris stated that the memoir was derived from a journal in which she wrote her reflections and recollections of "the shortest presidential campaign in modern history".
Harris collaborated with Geraldine Brooks to give the book a "novelistic feel", and Simon & Schuster CEO Jonathan Karp stated that the book reads more like a suspense novel than a political memoir.
On August 21, 2025, Harris announced on social media that she would be starting an international book tour in September 2025 to promote 107 Days.
She scheduled eighteen appearances in cities across the United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom; the first leg lasted through November 2025.
In contrast to Biden, Harris did have a more well-received debate performance with Trump.
The new memoir by the former vice president defends her campaign and allows others to criticize Joe Biden and his team for her failure to win.
"Kamala Harris slams Trump for pardoning 'the fentanyl dealer Ross Ulbricht'"."},{"url":"https://www.kamalaharristour.com/","title":"Kamala Harris Tour - 107 Days Book Tour","content":"Kamala Harris hits the road in 2026 for a national book tour in support of her new memoir, 107 Days."}]}
